briefdescription.para: The mutex to protect the instance.
The global instance of the PosixBackend.
The terminal flags.
If the platform was initialized.
If the backend is interactive.
If the backend saved the cursor state.
The current cursor visibility state.
The current input mode.
Flag if the alternate screen is active.
Queued decoded key events.
Buffered translated Unicode text input.
Stored first UTF-16 surrogate for the next event.
Helper that forwards termination events safely.
Windows specific variables.
Initialize the platform.

detaileddescription.para: This method is called from Terminal::initializeScreen().

briefdescription.para: Restore the platform.

detaileddescription.para: This method is called from Termina::restoreScreen().

briefdescription.para: Test if the platform supports color output.

detaileddescription.para:
This just tests for color support, not cursor movement. If you return false, no ANSI color codes are emitted and the line buffer is disabled. Instead, the emitColor() method is called on the backend.
Before each call to emitColor(), the text from the line buffer is emitted.

briefdescription.para: Test if the platform supports cursor movement.

detaileddescription.para:
This includes cursor position, clearing the terminal and other cursor-related operations. If you return false, no cursor movement commands are emitted and the line buffer is disabled. Instead, the moveCursor() etc. methods are called on the backend.
Before each call of the cursor methods, the text from the line buffer is emitted.

briefdescription.para: Test if the backend supports cursor visible/invisible ANSI sequences.

detaileddescription.para: If this method returns true, the ANSI codes ESC[?25l and ESC[?25h to control the cursor visibility. If it returns false, Terminal calls setCursorVisible() instead.

briefdescription.para: Test if the backend supports alternate screen buffer codes.

detaileddescription.para: If this method returns true, the ANSI codes ESC[?1049h and ESC[?1049l are sent to the backend to switch to the alternate screen buffer. Additionally setAlternateScreenBuffer() is called to notify the backend about the change. If this method returns false, Terminal only calls setAlternateScreenBuffer() instead.

briefdescription.para: Check if an interactive terminal is attached to the process.

detaileddescription.para:
This state is typically established during Terminal::initializeScreen().
true if interactive terminal features such as resize and cursor control are available.

briefdescription.para: Detect the current terminal screen size.

detaileddescription.para: This method is invoked by Terminal::initializeScreen() and Terminal::testScreenSize() when size detection is enabled. Interactive applications may call testScreenSize() before every screen update, so implementations should be efficient or perform updates asynchronously in the background.
The returned size should represent the visible terminal area available for output. A safety margin of one column and one row is applied by Terminal automatically.

simplesect.para: The detected screen size, or std::nullopt if detection failed.

briefdescription.para: Control if the cursor is visible on the terminal.

detaileddescription.para: If supportsCursorVisibilityCodes() returns false, this method is called to control the cursor visibility.

briefdescription.para: Emit the given UTF-8 encoded text.

detaileddescription.para:
If the backend does not support UTF-8, you are responsible to convert the text to the expected encoding. Line breaks are usually just NL and not CRLF. UTF-8 sequences, and ANSI sequences are always complete in one call of this method.
text
The UTF-8 encoded text to emit.

briefdescription.para: Flush the output buffer.

detaileddescription.para: After a call of this method, the backend must flush all previously emitted text and control sequences to the terminal.

briefdescription.para: Enables/disables the alternate screen buffer or notifies the backend about the change.
Get the current input mode.
Set the current input mode.
Read one key event without blocking longer than the given timeout.

detaileddescription.para:
In Input::Mode::Key, negative timeouts must be normalized to zero and any timeout less than or equal to zero must perform a non-blocking poll. In Input::Mode::ReadLine, the timeout is ignored and this call may block until a line was entered.
timeout
Maximum wait time in Mode::Key.
The parsed key event, or an invalid key if no supported input was read before the timeout expired.

briefdescription.para: Wait until one key event is available.

detaileddescription.para:
In Input::Mode::ReadLine, this call blocks until a line was entered and returns the converted key.
The parsed key event.

briefdescription.para: Read text input in the terminal.

detaileddescription.para:
Can be ignored in Input::Mode::Key mode.
The read text, without line breaks.

briefdescription.para: Create or access the global instance.
Access the global instance.
Restore the platform using the global instance.
Enabled UTF-8 in the Windows terminal.
Enables ANSI mode in the Windows terminal.
Change the cursor visibility.

parameterdescription.para: true to show the cursor, false to hide it

briefdescription.para: Queue a decoded key one or more times.
Flush one buffered Unicode text input into the key queue.
Append a translated Unicode code point to the buffered text input.
Decode one UTF-16 code unit from the console into a Unicode code point.
Read one key using either a timeout-based poll or a blocking wait.
Restore the terminal and terminate the process for one handled event.
Translate Windows control-key flags into key modifiers.
